sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain and swelling in his right knee following a skiing injury. He states he felt a pop during the incident. He describes the pain as exacerbated with flexion and extension. He reports mild bruising to his left shoulder following the fall. He denies any other injuries.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Vitals: Temperature 97.9°F, Heart Rate 64 bpm, Respiratory Rate 16 breaths/min, Blood Pressure 140/90 mmHg, Oxygen Saturation 98%. Examination reveals moderate edema and ecchymosis around the right knee. Range of motion is limited due to pain. Left shoulder demonstrates mild bruising. X-ray of the right knee reveals no fracture or bony abnormalities.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a right knee contusion. He reports pain with flexion and extension. There is no evidence of fracture.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I recommend rest, ice, compression, and elevation for the right knee. Prescribe ibuprofen 650mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain. Order a left shoulder X-ray to rule out further injury. Schedule a follow-up appointment in 7 days to assess progress and consider physical therapy if needed.
